stories.title: Pete Crow-Armstrong Hits for the Cycle — First of 2026, Cubs Win 5-4
stories.angle: Cubs center fielder Pete Crow-Armstrong, 24, hit for the cycle in reverse order (HR in 1st, 3B in 3rd, 2B in 5th, 1B in 7th) in a 5-4 Cubs win over the Rockies on June 15. He went 4-for-4 with 2 RBI. It was the first cycle of the 2026 MLB season, the 13th in Cubs franchise history, and the first-ever reverse-order cycle in Cubs history. Crow-Armstrong is the youngest Cub to hit for the cycle since Randy Hundley in 1966. The Cubs won on a walk-off bases-loaded walk by Matt Shaw in the 9th.

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
Pete Crow-Armstrong hit for the cycle Monday — but he did it backwards.

HR → 3B → 2B → 1B.

First reverse-order cycle in Cubs history. First cycle of the 2026 season. Youngest Cub to do it since Randy Hundley in 1966.

MVPETE had quite a day. #Cubs #MLB

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
The 2026 MLB season finally has its first cycle — and it came with a twist.

Pete Crow-Armstrong (age 24) hit for the cycle in reverse order vs. the Rockies on June 15.

4-for-4. 2 RBI. 13th cycle in Cubs history. The first in reverse order. #Cubs #MLB

facebook.long_form:
Monday night at Wrigley — well, technically it started in the afternoon — Pete Crow-Armstrong did something no Chicago Cub had ever done before.

The 24-year-old center fielder hit for the cycle against the Colorado Rockies on June 15. That's impressive enough on its own. But he did it in reverse order: home run in the first inning, triple in the third, double in the fifth, and single in the seventh.

Not just the first cycle of the 2026 MLB season. Not just the 13th cycle in Cubs franchise history. The first reverse-order cycle in Cubs history. And only the fourth player in the entire divisional era — going back to 1969 — to accomplish the feat in reverse order.

Crow-Armstrong, who went 4-for-4 with two RBI, said afterward he felt upset with himself for getting picked off immediately after recording the single that completed the cycle. "I did everything I could to help the team," he told reporters, "but I also had a real lapse in focus."

The game still needed him. The Cubs trailed 4-3 entering the ninth before Matt Shaw drew a bases-loaded walk to send Chicago home with a 5-4 victory. The win was part of a stretch that has Cubs fans starting to wonder: is this the year MVPETE puts it all together?

At 24, he is the youngest Cubs player to hit for the cycle since catcher Randy Hundley did it in 1966. Whatever frustrations come with the inconsistency, performances like Monday's are a reminder of what this player can do when everything clicks.
facebook.image_caption: Pete Crow-Armstrong hit for the cycle in REVERSE ORDER on June 15 — HR, 3B, 2B, 1B. First cycle of the 2026 season. First reverse-order cycle in Cubs history. 13th cycle overall in franchise history. MVPETE had a day. #Cubs #MLB

article.headline: Pete Crow-Armstrong Hits for the Cycle in Reverse Order — First of the 2026 Season

stories.title: Eduardo Rodriguez Earns 100th Career Win — D-Backs 8-1 Angels
stories.angle: Arizona Diamondbacks starter Eduardo Rodriguez pitched 7 IP, 6 H, 1 ER, 3 BB, 5 K to earn his 100th career win as the D-Backs beat the Angels 8-1 on June 17. Rodriguez lowered his ERA to 2.45 for the 2026 season. He became the 9th Venezuelan-born pitcher in MLB history to win 100 career games. Corbin Carroll hit a grand slam in the game.

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
Eduardo Rodriguez: career win No. 100.

7 IP, 1 ER, 5 K. D-Backs beat the Angels 8-1. Season ERA: 2.45.

E-Rod is the 9th Venezuelan-born pitcher in MLB history to reach 100 wins.

Corbin Carroll hit a grand slam. Good day in Arizona. #Dbacks #MLB

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
Eduardo Rodriguez. 100 career wins.

7 innings. 5 strikeouts. 1 earned run. 2.45 ERA on the season.

He's had stints with the Red Sox, Tigers, and Yankees. He's made it to 100 in Arizona.

The 9th Venezuelan pitcher in MLB history to do it. #Dbacks

facebook.long_form:
Eduardo Rodriguez has been to Boston, Detroit, and New York. He's had injuries that kept him from the mound for entire seasons. He's switched teams, rebuilt his craft, and earned his place at the front of a rotation more than once.

On Wednesday night in Anaheim, all of that came together in one number: 100.

E-Rod threw seven innings against the Los Angeles Angels, allowing just one run on six hits and walking three while striking out five. The Arizona Diamondbacks won 8-1, with Corbin Carroll providing a grand slam for good measure. And Eduardo Rodriguez picked up career win No. 100 — his most significant milestone as a professional.

The achievement places Rodriguez in rarefied company: he is the ninth Venezuelan-born pitcher in the history of Major League Baseball to win 100 games. The list of predecessors includes some celebrated names in the sport's history.

His 2026 season has been quietly excellent. A 2.45 ERA across his starts for Arizona is the work of a pitcher who has found consistency at the back end of his prime years. He was good in Boston, had his moments with Detroit, but in Arizona he has found a home and a rotation where he belongs.

Win No. 100 is the proof.
facebook.image_caption: Eduardo Rodriguez earned career win No. 100 on Wednesday — 7 IP, 1 ER, 5 K, 2.45 ERA on the season. The 9th Venezuelan-born pitcher in MLB history to reach the milestone. Corbin Carroll hit a grand slam. Good day in Arizona. #Dbacks #MLB

article.headline: Eduardo Rodriguez Earns Career Win No. 100 — 9th Venezuelan Pitcher to Reach the Milestone

stories.title: Freeman Go-Ahead HR, Ohtani Shaky — Dodgers Sweep Rays 5-4
stories.angle: Freddie Freeman hit a go-ahead two-run homer (407 feet, center field) in the 6th inning off Kevin Kelly, rescuing a banged-up Shohei Ohtani who gave up 4 ER in the 5th. The Dodgers won 5-4 and completed a three-game sweep of the Rays — their first sweep of Tampa Bay since August 2013. Freeman's 20th career walk-off related hit.

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
Ohtani gave up 4 runs in the 5th. The Dodgers trailed 4-2.

Then Freddie Freeman hit a 407-foot two-run shot in the 6th to take the lead.

Dodgers 5, Rays 4. Series sweep. First Dodgers sweep of Tampa Bay since 2013.

Freddie just has that aura. #Dodgers #MLB

facebook.long_form:
The Dodgers were in trouble Wednesday night. Shohei Ohtani — their $700M man, their ace, their franchise cornerstone — gave up four earned runs in the fifth inning against the Tampa Bay Rays. Los Angeles trailed 4-2. The Rays smelled an upset.

Then Freddie Freeman stepped up.

Freeman launched a two-run home run 407 feet to center field in the sixth inning off reliever Kevin Kelly. The shot turned a 4-2 deficit into a 5-4 lead. The Dodgers didn't look back. The bullpen held. Los Angeles won 5-4 and completed a three-game sweep of the Rays — just the second time in franchise history the Dodgers have swept Tampa Bay, and the first since August 2013.

MLB.com called it perfectly: "Freddie just has that aura." There's something about Freeman in big moments. He does this consistently — rescues starts, adds insurance runs, delivers clutch hits at the moment a game tips one way or the other.

Ohtani will be fine. A rough inning against Tampa Bay is a blip in the context of his career. But Wednesday belonged to Freeman, and the sweep belonged to the Dodgers.
facebook.image_caption: Ohtani gave up 4 runs in the 5th. The Dodgers were down 4-2. Then Freddie Freeman hit a 407-foot two-run shot in the 6th. Dodgers 5, Rays 4. Sweep complete. First Dodgers sweep of Tampa Bay since 2013. Freddie just has that aura. #Dodgers #MLB

article.headline: Freeman's Go-Ahead Homer Rescues Ohtani — Dodgers Complete Sweep of Rays

stories.title: Schwarber Leads NL with 23 HR — Derby Countdown at Citizens Bank
stories.angle: Kyle Schwarber leads the NL with 23 HR and a .949 OPS. Yordan Alvarez leads the AL. Both are prime Derby candidates for the July 13 event at Citizens Bank Park — Schwarber's home ballpark. CBS Sports ideal field includes Schwarber, Alvarez, Harper, Trout, and Rice. Phase 1 All-Star voting closes June 25.

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
Kyle Schwarber leads the NL with 23 HR and a .949 OPS.

Yordan Alvarez leads the AL.

The Home Run Derby is July 13 at Citizens Bank Park — Schwarber's home yard.

This is the matchup the Derby was made for. #Phillies #Astros #MLB

facebook.long_form:
The Home Run Derby is coming to Citizens Bank Park on July 13 — and nobody on the planet has a bigger rooting interest in what that Derby field looks like than Kyle Schwarber.

Schwarber, the Phillies designated hitter, is currently leading the National League in home runs (23), slugging percentage (.598), and OPS (.949). He is everything the Derby was built for: a left-handed pull hitter with elite power, playing in a park that has given up more home runs than almost any other in the league. When the Derby comes to your home ballpark, you want to be there.

On the American League side, Yordan Alvarez continues to pound the ball for the Houston Astros. Alvarez and Schwarber have been running neck-and-neck in the home run race for most of the season, separated by no more than a handful at any point.

CBS Sports has already put together its ideal Derby field: Schwarber, Alvarez, Bryce Harper, Mike Trout, and Ben Rice. That would be a television event. Philadelphia fans packing Citizens Bank for the Derby would have the additional drama of watching Schwarber compete in front of the most passionate home crowd in baseball.

The Derby invitations haven't been formally sent. The field announcement is coming. But the Schwarber-Alvarez storyline is already written — the question is just how many home runs they hit before July 13.
facebook.image_caption: Kyle Schwarber leads the NL with 23 HR and a .949 OPS. The Home Run Derby comes to Citizens Bank Park on July 13. CBS Sports' ideal field: Schwarber, Alvarez, Harper, Trout, Rice. This is going to be something. #Phillies #MLB

article.headline: Schwarber Leads NL with 23 HRs — The Derby at Citizens Bank Is the Setting He Was Made For

stories.title: All-Star Ballot Update — Ohtani Dominates, Phase 1 Closes June 25
stories.angle: Shohei Ohtani leads all MLB players in All-Star voting. Phase 1 ends June 25 at noon ET — the leading vote-getter in each league earns an automatic starting spot. Baldwin (C) leads catchers. All-Star Game is July 14 at Citizens Bank Park in Philadelphia.

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
All-Star voting Phase 1 closes June 25.

Ohtani leads all players. Drake Baldwin leads catchers.

The leading vote-getter in each league gets an AUTOMATIC starting spot.

All-Star Game: July 14 at Citizens Bank Park in Philadelphia. Go vote. #MLB #AllStarGame

facebook.long_form:
The first phase of 2026 MLB All-Star voting closes on June 25, and the stakes are real: the player with the most votes in each league at the end of Phase 1 earns an automatic starting spot — no Phase 2 needed.

As of the latest update, Shohei Ohtani leads all players in voting. He is the most popular baseball player on the planet, and the numbers reflect it. Over on the American League leaderboard, Yordan Alvarez leads the DH position.

Drake Baldwin, the Atlanta Braves catcher who has been one of the breakout performers of the 2026 season, leads the catcher position in voting with over 972,000 ballots cast. Baldwin's All-Star case has been building alongside his offensive numbers all year — a monster bat, plus the national stage of a team that has been playing meaningful games deep into the first half.

First baseman Freddie Freeman leads among NL first basemen, while Matt Olson trails him in the Atlanta market.

Phase 1 ends at noon ET on Thursday, June 25. Phase 2 begins June 29. The 2026 All-Star Game itself is July 14 at Citizens Bank Park in Philadelphia — the same venue hosting the Home Run Derby the night before on July 13. It is shaping up to be one of the great mid-summer weekends in recent baseball memory.
facebook.image_caption: All-Star voting Phase 1 closes June 25 at noon ET. Leading vote-getters in each league get AUTOMATIC starting spots. Ohtani leads all players. Baldwin leads catchers. All-Star Game: July 14 in Philadelphia. Go vote. #MLB #AllStarGame

article.headline: All-Star Ballot: Ohtani Leads All Players, Phase 1 Closes June 25 — Here's What's at Stake
